critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: REQ-005 still says fuel is required only at volunteer acceptance, contradicting Decision-8's project funding from draft onward and pre-publish Discovery funding path.
critique.evidence: line 698: "fuel is required only at volunteer-acceptance time"; line 717: "the 'funded only at volunteer acceptance' rule is dropped" and "any point from `draft` onward".

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The visibility default is ambiguous for Tier-2 projects because REQ-005 defaults every scope doc to `public_mit` while REQ-023 says Tier-2 projects default to private.
critique.evidence: line 702: "default `public_mit`, opt-in to `private`"; line 1352: "Tier-2 projects also default to `visibility = 'private'`."

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The scope document fields listed in REQ-005 omit required Discovery and triage inputs, so builders cannot tell which fields are displayed, editable, persisted, or submitted for review.
critique.evidence: line 701 lists only "summary, user stories, acceptance criteria, suggested stack"; REQ-004 defines `riskFlags`, `dataSensitivity`, `maintenanceTrack`, `suggestedLovable`, and `recommendedWorkflow` at lines 574-607; REQ-023 triage requires `dataSensitivity`, fixtures-only plan, and `risk_flags` at lines 1352-1353.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The Dependencies line omits real dependencies used normatively by the section, causing lifecycle, funding, verification, and marketplace work to be under-specified for Linear decomposition.
critique.evidence: line 711 lists only "REQ-004, REQ-023" while the section uses REQ-006 funding at lines 698/701/704, lifecycle transitions from REQ-005.5 at lines 703/706/708-709, `verified` NGO status from REQ-002 at line 705, and `/marketplace` behavior at line 707.

blocking_questions: Can a project have funded fuel before publishing, and if so how should the scope/publish UI represent prior fuel and later match funding?
For Tier-2 projects, should the scope editor automatically default visibility to private, and is a separate NGO-written `visibility_justification` still required?
Which DiscoveryOutput fields are part of the published scope document, which are editable by the NGO, and which are read-only metadata for triage?
What exact side effects occur when an NGO unpublishes an open project with pending applications, and which requirement owns the notifications and application retention?

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The submit criterion contradicts the lifecycle table by saying intake submission always transitions to `discovery_in_progress`, while the lifecycle requires email verification and available Discovery capacity or the project stays `draft`.
critique.evidence: REQ-003 lines 523-525; REQ-005.5 line 647.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The Dependencies line omits the real lifecycle and Discovery dependencies needed to implement submission behavior, conversation start, capacity checks, and failure states.
critique.evidence: REQ-003 line 528 lists only REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-032; REQ-004 line 534 and REQ-005.5 lines 640-647 define the coupled behavior.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: Intake-time file upload ownership is circular and not decomposable because REQ-003 includes upload as an acceptance criterion while REQ-032 also owns uploads and depends on REQ-003.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: `estimated urgency` is required in the form but has no declared persistence location or downstream use in the core project schema, so builders may add a column, hide it in `intake_raw`, or drop it.
critique.evidence: REQ-003 line 522; projects schema lines 2267-2320 includes `title`, `intake_raw`, and `cause_tags` but no urgency field.

critique.label: local
critique.issue: `raw intake` is not defined precisely enough for auditing because it does not say whether it includes all form fields, file metadata, timestamps, original markdown, or an immutable `audit_events` entry.
critique.evidence: REQ-003 line 526; REQ-005.5 line 647 says `audit_events` logs intake; projects schema line 2317 only has `intake_raw TEXT`.

critique.label: local
critique.issue: The cause-tag multi-select has no canonical taxonomy or source of options, yet later marketplace matching relies on cause overlap.
critique.evidence: REQ-003 line 522; marketplace overlap behavior at lines 989-991.

blocking_questions: Should REQ-003 submission obey REQ-005.5 by remaining `draft` when email verification or Discovery capacity is missing?
Is `estimated urgency` a persisted project field, part of `intake_raw`, or not part of v1?
Is intake-time reference upload implemented inside the REQ-003 Linear issue or wholly delegated to REQ-032?
What is the canonical cause-tag taxonomy/source for the multi-select?

critique.label: unmade-decision
critique.issue: The third-rejection platform-review and volunteer-contest path commits dispute authority without a traceable decision-ledger entry or defined outcomes.
critique.evidence: loop/out/prd.working.md:1189: "after 2 rejectâ†’resubmit cycles, the third rejection routes to neutral platform review ... the volunteer has a 'contest this rejection' path."

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: Repo handoff permissions contradict REQ-008 for the v1 Lovable path, so implementers cannot know whether the volunteer keeps triage access or is removed.
critique.evidence: REQ-012 line 1191 says "volunteer downgraded to `triage`"; REQ-008 line 1061 says "ai4good removes the volunteer's collaborator role at handoff."

critique.label: local
critique.issue: The automated handoff checklist is not fully pass/fail because it does not define the deploy-instructions location or whether the passing CI run must be for the current `main` HEAD.
critique.evidence: loop/out/prd.working.md:1183: "deploy instructions section present, â‰¥1 passing CI run on `main`."

critique.label: local
critique.issue: The Track-A handoff ritual has human-action gates that lack verification rules, conditionality, and required recorded artifacts.
critique.evidence: loop/out/prd.working.md:1185-1186 require enabling Supabase RLS, demoing rollback, setting a Lovable spend cap/budget alert, and confirming two-way GitHub sync without specifying how each is proven or stored.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: REQ-012 omits real dependencies needed to implement its lifecycle side effects and notifications, especially REQ-005.5, REQ-016, REQ-014, and REQ-030.
critique.evidence: loop/out/prd.working.md:1190 awards completion credit, removes Linear membership, commits a snapshot, and terminates keys; line 1194 dependencies list only REQ-008, REQ-006, REQ-021, REQ-009, REQ-026, REQ-035.

blocking_questions: What authority does neutral platform review have after the third rejection or a volunteer contest: force accept, send back to work, cancel, or some other terminal/nonterminal outcome?
For v1 Lovable repo handoff, should the volunteer retain `triage` access or be removed from the repo entirely?

section: REQ-010: Project Page (single view) & Cadence Stats

critique.label: unmade-decision
critique.issue: REQ-010 fixes the project/status-panel surface even though the document explicitly leaves that exact surface open as OD-2.
critique.evidence: REQ-026/Open Decisions: "What the panel shows beyond the tree, and how the NGO is introduced to it: [DECISION: OD-2 - NGO Status-Panel scope + onboarding mechanics]"; REQ-010 nevertheless specifies task tree, activity feed, reference files, clarifications, fuel meters, and cadence stats.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The Dependencies line omits real build dependencies that REQ-010 directly relies on, so implementers would not pull the same prerequisite issues.
critique.evidence: REQ-010 declares only "Dependencies: REQ-008, REQ-009" while its ACs reference REQ-026, REQ-032, REQ-024, REQ-021, REQ-007, REQ-013, and REQ-014.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The page-public rule does not define lifecycle visibility, creating a possible leak of draft, discovery, scoped, or triage project data before marketplace approval.
critique.evidence: REQ-010: "Page is public (logged-out users can view)"; REQ-005 says only "Published-and-approved projects appear on /marketplace with status open" and REQ-023 routes projects through triage first.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The activity-feed translation assumes every commit has a task reference, but the Lovable path explicitly makes Linear identifiers best-effort and non-load-bearing.
critique.evidence: REQ-010: "Translation derives from commit-message task-references and PM task titles"; REQ-021: "Knowledge snippet is provided as a best-effort signal (not load-bearing)" and Lovable commits depend on "best-effort commit-prefix convention".

critique.label: local
critique.issue: The section says there are no per-role variants, but its own ACs require role-specific data, downloads, CTAs, and NGO-only file mutation controls.
critique.evidence: REQ-010: "One view; no toggle; no per-role variants" versus "Download is available to the assigned volunteer + NGO admins + platform admin" and "The NGO can add/remove files here pre-handoff."

blocking_questions: Has OD-2 been resolved, and what exact v1 project/status-panel surface is approved beyond the Linear task tree?
Which lifecycle states have a logged-out public project page, and what data is hidden before triage approval?
How should the activity feed render commits without Linear task references, especially Lovable bot commits?

critique.label: local
critique.issue: The section defines incompatible role models, so implementers cannot know whether authz is based on org-scoped `ngo_admin`/`volunteer`/`platform_admin`, profile-level `user_type`, or membership roles `admin`/`member`.

critique.label: local
critique.issue: The teammate invitation requirement cannot be implemented consistently because the section specifies only final memberships and omits the pending invite model, expiry, acceptance, resend, revoke, and existing-user versus new-user behavior.
critique.evidence: Line 411 requires email invitation and role assignment; lines 470-475 define only `ngo_memberships`.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The blanket RLS criterion conflicts with required public project visibility and platform-admin access, so a builder could incorrectly hide public pages or block admin review queues.
critique.evidence: Line 413 says NGO data is visible only to members and assigned volunteers; lines 1128-1131 require public project pages, and line 1366 restricts triage queue access to `platform_admin`.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: Email verification is modeled inconsistently between profile-level verification and an NGO-level lifecycle gate, leaving the Discovery bot-floor check ambiguous.
critique.evidence: Lines 431-434 and 495 use `profiles.email_verified_at` / at least one admin; line 647 gates on `ngos.email_verified_at`.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: REQ-001 embeds NGO verification and Discovery-credit schema owned by REQ-002/REQ-004/REQ-006 while declaring no dependencies, making the Linear split between auth, NGO profile, and Discovery credits non-deterministic.
critique.evidence: Lines 443-466 define `verification_state` and `discovery_credits_remaining` with REQ-002/REQ-004/REQ-006 references; line 480 says `Dependencies: None`.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The `account_status` lifecycle fields are present but their required write-rejection behavior is not an acceptance criterion in this section, even though later requirements depend on REQ-001 enforcing it.
critique.evidence: Lines 424-430 define `account_status`; line 981 says mutation middleware in REQ-026/REQ-001 checks it on every write.

blocking_questions: What is the canonical authorization model: profile-level `user_type`, NGO membership `role`, or org-scoped roles named `ngo_admin`, `volunteer`, and `platform_admin`?
What exact RLS helper predicates should apply to public project pages, private project data, platform-admin queues, NGO members, and assigned volunteers?
Is email verification a profile-level property checked for at least one NGO admin, or a denormalized NGO-level property used by lifecycle gates?
What persistent invite model is required for NGO teammate invitations, including expiry, acceptance, revoke/resend, and role assignment?

section: REQ-002: NGO Organization Profile + Verification (v1 minimal)

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The dependency line is materially incomplete, so Linear decomposition from this section would miss required Discovery, funding, lifecycle, and card-surface integrations.
critique.evidence: Line 513 declares only `REQ-001`, while the section depends on Discovery credits/credit reset (lines 490, 502), Stripe fuel continuation (line 490), the `scoped -> triage` publish gate (line 500), and marketplace/project-card badges (line 503).

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The org profile field set is inconsistent with the declared dependency schema and user-story surface.
critique.evidence: Line 494 requires `name, mission, country, website, logo`; REQ-001 `ngos` schema only defines `name`, `mission`, and `website` at lines 438-442, while Story 1 also requires `contact` at line 237.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The Discovery email-verification gate has two incompatible data models.
critique.evidence: Line 495 says `profiles.email_verified_at` for at least one NGO admin unlocks Discovery, but REQ-005.5 line 647 gates `draft -> discovery_in_progress` on `ngos.email_verified_at IS NOT NULL`.

critique.label: local
critique.issue: The verification evidence required for `verified` is not decomposable because the tier definition requires a public reference link but the acceptance criteria only implement a PDF upload and doc review.
critique.evidence: Line 491 says verified means `Submitted registration document + public reference link`, while lines 496 and 499 only specify registration document upload and an admin doc viewer.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The verified-credit transition conflicts with a downstream task hint that would cause a different credit refill implementation.
critique.evidence: Line 498 sets today's remaining credits to 30 on `unverified -> verified`, but line 2744 still says `unverifiedâ†’verified 500-refill`.

blocking_questions: What is the canonical Discovery email gate: the acting admin's `profiles.email_verified_at`, any current NGO admin's `profiles.email_verified_at`, or an org-level email field?
Does v1 verification require both a registration PDF and a public reference link, and where is the reference link collected and reviewed?
Should the NGO profile schema include `country`, `logo`, and `contact`, or are any of those intentionally deferred or derived elsewhere?
On verification, is the same-day credit value capped/set to 30 or is any legacy 500-refill behavior still intended?

section: REQ-009: LLM Gateway â€” Virtual Keys, Caps & Inline Fuel Metering (decision-21)

critique.label: unmade-decision
critique.issue: Gateway implementation cannot be decomposed because the runtime and hosting architecture is explicitly undecided.
critique.evidence: Line 1069: "Gateway hosting is an OPEN decision â€” [DECISION: OD-6 ... Supabase Edge Functions vs a self-hosted LiteLLM-style service]"; the Open Decisions register says OD-6 blocks REQ-009 Task 9.1.

critique.label: unmade-decision
critique.issue: The cap and fingerprint acceptance criteria are not pass/fail testable because they depend on unresolved numeric decisions.
critique.evidence: REQ-009 specifies caps "sized 3-4x an expected heavy human day" with [DECISION: OD-3] and fingerprint enforcement "above ~1.5k input tokens" with [DECISION: OD-4].

critique.label: local
critique.issue: Virtual-key authentication is underspecified because a bcrypt-only `key_hash` plus an `a4g_*` prefix gives the gateway no indexed lookup path for the presented key.
critique.evidence: Line 1092 defines `key_hash` as bcrypt with plaintext shown once; schema line 850 defines `virtual_keys` without a key id, deterministic lookup digest, or keyed prefix column.

critique.label: local
critique.issue: The fuel gate can still overspend a project with a small positive balance because pricing happens after the streamed response and no reservation or max-cost check is specified.
critique.evidence: Line 1098 prices and writes the ledger "per gateway response"; line 1100 only says "0% -> the gateway declines the next request inline".

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: REQ-009 contradicts still-live build requirements that tell implementers to build the retired Anthropic Usage API poller and key-deactivation flow.
critique.evidence: Line 1069 deletes the Usage-Report poller and key-deactivation saga, but Story 4 lines 323-324 still require Usage API polling and key deactivation at 5%/0%.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The inline ledger AC imports an inconsistent pair invariant, so implementers cannot determine the required row amounts for `consumption` and `skim`.
critique.evidence: Line 1098 says to write paired `consumption` + `skim` rows with all REQ-006 invariants unchanged, while line 756 says rows for a `pair_id` "must sum to zero" and line 758 defines `skim = fuel_debit - anthropic_cost`.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The declared dependency line omits requirements needed to implement REQ-009's own ACs, especially repo-template fingerprint seeding, Linear policy prompt content, and notifications.
critique.evidence: Line 1096 requires `.a4g/fingerprint.md` in the repo template, line 1097 references the Linear status rule, but line 1122 dependencies omit REQ-008/REQ-021/REQ-026/REQ-016.

blocking_questions: Which gateway hosting/runtime is chosen for v1: Supabase Edge Functions or a self-hosted gateway service?
What exact per-request max-token and rolling-24h cap defaults, warning thresholds, and override bounds should Task 9.3 implement?
What exact fingerprint threshold and marker format should Task 9.4 implement?
What is the virtual-key lookup format: key id prefix, deterministic digest, HMAC lookup column, or something else?
Must the gateway reserve or reject based on maximum possible request cost before streaming, or is one-response fuel overrun allowed?
What exact ledger row shape and sign convention satisfies both `consumption` + `skim` accounting and the pair-sum invariant?
Are the old Story 4 Usage API polling and key-deactivation requirements deleted or still required somewhere outside REQ-009?

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The Dependencies line declares only REQ-003 even though REQ-004 materially depends on verification/credits, fuel ledger routing, attachments, triage, Lovable provisioning, lifecycle, and handoff behavior.
critique.evidence: line 636 says "Dependencies: REQ-003" while lines 534, 540, 543-544, 547, and 562 rely on REQ-002/006/032/023/021/005.5/012.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: Tier-2 handling is contradictory: the section and triage path allow Tier-2 Track-A projects with synthetic fixtures, but the system prompt later says Tier-2-data tools are Track B and waitlisted.
critique.evidence: lines 543 and 632 describe Tier-2 fixtures-only build guidance, REQ-023 line 1352 allows Tier-2 after triage, but line 616 says "Tier-2-data tools are Track B -> waitlist."

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: The Track-B waitlist path is not decomposable because no lifecycle transition, status, table, notification, or ownership is defined for a Discovery that must not emit a publishable scope.
critique.evidence: line 544 says non-Track-A needs "do not get a publishable scope" and are recorded on a waitlist, while REQ-005.5 line 648 only defines discovery_in_progress -> scoped when valid DiscoveryOutput JSON is produced.

critique.label: cross-section
critique.issue: Publishable v1 Discovery output can still imply Lovable is optional, conflicting with the v1 invariant that every built project is Track A and Lovable setup is mandatory.
critique.evidence: lines 600-602 define suggestedLovable.needed: boolean and line 621 renders Lovable only when true, while line 2308 says v1 lovable_recommended is always true and line 1293 requires Lovable setup at kickoff.

critique.label: local
critique.issue: The no-charge retry/regeneration rule is ambiguous for funded projects because the section says retries are free credits, but funded Discovery says every turn with positive project balance debits fuel.
critique.evidence: line 545 says regeneration/system-error retries are "FREE (cost 0 Discovery credits)"; line 547 says if project fuel balance is positive, the turn debits project fuel.

critique.label: local
critique.issue: The large-file ingestion confirmation is not pass/fail testable because "large" has no threshold or platform setting, so implementers can gate different file sizes or token estimates.
critique.evidence: line 548 requires a prompt "before the agent ingests a large uploaded file" but defines no size/token threshold.

blocking_questions: When Discovery classifies a need as Track B in v1, what exact project status/table records the waitlist and does the original project remain discovery_in_progress, become cancelled, or become another state?
Are Tier-2 projects allowed in v1 as Track A with synthetic fixtures, or must all Tier-2-data tools be waitlisted as Track B?
For a publishable v1 DiscoveryOutput, can suggestedLovable.needed ever be false?
For funded Discovery retries/regenerations, should the system write zero ledger rows, write audit-only rows, or refund/waive a normal discovery_consumption charge?
